title: First public records request sent.
summary: The coalition sent its first public records request focused on Ordinance 2024-07, the May 20, 2024 Special Council Meeting record, and the Jahn Property Industrial Major Site Plan.
what_happened: FXF-PR-2026-001 was delivered to the Village Fiscal Officer, with copies to the Zoning Administrator and the Mayor.
why_it_matters: The records this request returns will determine whether procedural and zoning questions can be reviewed before any further approvals.
what_to_do: Sign up for updates and indicate records research in the volunteer field if you can help review documents when they return.

title: Council meets on May 4. Public comment is open.
summary: The next regular council meeting is May 4, 2026. Public comment is accepted at the start of the meeting.
what_happened: Council regular meeting at the Village Hall in Commercial Point. Agenda packet expected on the village calendar a few days before.
why_it_matters: Two data-center items are likely to be discussed: the proposed eighteen-month moratorium and the K-Nova site plan record. Council attendance is the single highest-leverage civic action.
what_to_do: Plan to attend in person or watch the livestream. If you cannot attend, email the council before the meeting.

title: Site launches at foxfirecoalition.org.
summary: The Homes of Foxfire Coalition Against Data Centers website is live with a public-record timeline, a documents library, education resources, and a take-action page.
what_happened: The first version of the site is published. Content is organized into six pages: Home, Timeline, Learn, Documents, Take Action, and Updates.
why_it_matters: Until now, residents had no single place to find the public record on the K-Nova / Jahn Farm data-center issue. The site is intended as that place.
what_to_do: Read the timeline. Sign up for updates. Share the link with a neighbor.
